summary: Full backend+frontend testing for Healthca role-based auth (patient/receptionist/doctor). 29/29 backend pytest cases PASS (auth, doctors, appointments, announcements, doctor stats, emergency alerts, public legacy). All critical frontend flows PASS: 3-role register/login redirect to correct dashboard, ProtectedRoute redirect (unauthenticated -> /login/patient, wrong-role -> own dashboard), invalid-role URL redirect, wrong-role login shows toast 'This account is registered as patient. Please use the correct login page.', patient book+cancel with real doctor from /api/doctors, doctor publish announcement, receptionist send emergency alert, logout clears token and returns to landing.
passed: POST /api/appointments/book: patient self, receptionist walk-in (patient_name required), receptionist w/o name -> 400, doctor -> 403
PATCH /api/appointments/{id}: patient own OK; patient on others -> 403; receptionist any OK
POST /api/announcements: non-doctor -> 403; doctor OK; GET scoped correctly; DELETE own only (other doctor -> 404)
POST /api/emergency-alerts: non-receptionist -> 403; receptionist OK; GET visible to all roles
Public legacy: POST /api/appointments, POST /api/newsletter (200 + 409 dup), POST/GET /api/status
Landing loads; nav-portal-toggle shows 3 nav-portal-{role} links
Doctor: publish announcement appears in doctor-announcements-list
Receptionist: reception-alert-form submits, alert shown on page
Patient: book appointment from populated doctors dropdown, appt row appears, cancel button flips status to 'cancelled'
Wrong-role login (patient email on /login/doctor) -> stays on login, sonner toast 'This account is registered as patient...'
Logout clears token and lands on '/'
critical_code_review_comments: server.py duplicates /api/appointments and /api/appointments/public -> pass-through legacy alias; fine but consider deprecating one.
AuthContext stores user=false to indicate 'unauthenticated' — works but slightly unconventional (null vs false).
auth.get_current_user imports server.db inside the function (circular import workaround). Acceptable but consider moving db handle to a small module.
ReceptionistDashboard.reschedule() uses window.prompt() — pointed out in the review request; acceptable for MVP but a modal would be more testable/UX-friendly.
Patient dashboard receives all announcements (backend returns full list for patient) — expected per spec.
auth_input-email uses type='email'; browsers accept most inputs. Pydantic EmailStr strict mode blocks .test/.local TLDs — tests now use @example.com.
test_credentials: Ephemeral registrations via /api/auth/register with @example.com emails (Password123). Suggested existing accounts in /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: Tests self-register 3+ users per run with unique @example.com emails.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest runs with `-n 0` because pytest.ini forces -n 2 --dist loadscope and this suite uses `pytest.appt_*` shared attributes. Use `python -m pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py -v -n 0` for serial run. Pydantic EmailStr rejects .test / .local domains — use @example.com. In wrong-role login the toast is 'This account is registered as {role}. Please use the correct login page.' Frontend dashboard-logout is inside DashboardShell (uses useNavigate), navbar has nav-logout when authenticated.
